2: You find Nonotta in the aetheryte plaza, boasting an adorable pair of chicken sunglasses and no little consternation. Apparently, the arena the Dreamers thought to use for their egg hunt has been overrun by a plant so stubborn it cannot be trimmed or culled, leading Nonotta to believe the flora may be a variation of potentially dangerous leafkin. Accepting a piece of the peculiar plant, you head to the Carline Canopy to question visitors on their knowledge of its source, while Nonotta heads for the Botanists' Guild.

You find Nonotta in the aetheryte plaza, bearing an adorable pair of chicken spectacles and no little consternation. Apparently, the egg hunting arena has been overrun by a plant so stubborn it cannot be trimmed or culled, leading Nonotta to wonder if the flora may be potentially dangerous leafkin. Accepting a piece of the peculiar plant for reference, you head to the Carline Canopy to question patrons, while Nonotta heads for the Botanists' Guild.

3: The leaf in your possession seems likely to belong to a strain of flytrap that easily regenerates upon being cut, and you are not the first person to inquire about bizarre plantlife─an eccentric alchemist came seeking similar information not long before you did. Having learned all you can, you return to the aetheryte plaza to share your findings with Nonotta.

The leaf in your possession seems likely to belong to a strain of flytrap that easily regenerates, and you are not the first to inquire about such plant life─an “eccentric” alchemist seems to have preceded you. Having learned all you can, you return to the aetheryte plaza to share your findings with Nonotta.

4: The Botanists' Guild has determined that the leafkin in question is so resilient that the pieces in your possession are technically still alive. Shelving that disturbing notion for the time being, you share what you discovered with Nonotta, and the two of you agree that following up with the eccentric alchemist seems as promising a lead as any.

The Botanists' Guild has determined that the leafkin in question is so resilient that the pieces in your possession are technically still alive. Shelving that disturbing notion for the time being, you share what you discovered with Nonotta, and the two of you decide to follow up with the alchemist.

5: Herberin the alchemist is as eccentric as rumor holds, but passionately offers his assistance in creating a concoction that might help combat the stubborn leafkin. Indeed, he has barely finished agreeing before he dashes off to purchase the necessary ingredients, Nonotta trailing in his wake.

Herberin, the indubitably eccentric alchemist, passionately offers his assistance in creating a concoction that might help combat the stubborn leafkin. Indeed, he has barely finished agreeing before he dashes off to purchase the necessary ingredients, Nonotta trailing in his wake.

8: Many a stalk has fallen by your blade. Speak with Nonotta to debrief from your mission.
Your blade has tasted leafkin flesh. Speak with Nonotta to debrief from your mission.

9: Nonotta praises you for a job well done and Herberin seems delighted with not only the results of his alchemical solution, but the copious amounts of floral samples your mowing has produced. As the Elezen skips off to study to his heart's content, you and Nonotta report back to Jihli Aliapoh to let her know the current state of Hatching–tide affairs.

Nonotta praises you for a job well done and Herberin seems delighted with not only the results of his alchemical solution, but the copious amounts of floral samples your mowing has produced. As the Elezen skips off to study to his heart's content, you report back to Jihli Aliapoh to reassure her that Hatching–tide festivities may carry on as planned.

10:
Jihli is surprised to hear of the trouble regarding the arena, but glad that the leafkin problem has been resolved, freeing up the arena for the egg hunt that will reenact her prophetic dream. Just as she expresses a wish to thank Herberin for his assistance in the matter, the alchemist in question comes tearing in, proclaiming a profound discovery. Not only has he managed to cure the injury inflicted upon his eyes by the very leafkin you just finished culling, but he claims great promise in its properties of regeneration. Alchemy as he knows it may be entering a golden age, and this, Jihli realizes, is the true meaning behind her dream, the golden eggs a representation of rising hopes for the future.

Thanks to your efforts, Hatching–tide festivities may now begin in earnest, but the egg arena may need a trim every now and again. Stop by for Sprig Cleaning whenever you have a moment, and enjoy the rest of these spring celebrations.

Jihli is surprised to hear of the troublesome leafkin, but glad that the arena is fit for preparing an egg hunt. Just as she expresses a wish to thank Herberin for his assistance, the alchemist barrels into the amphitheatre, hollering success. His eyes, once brutally burned by leafkin venom, have been cured thanks to a solution concocted from the regenerative leafkin samples you provided him. The optimism he expresses for what this breakthrough means for the world of alchemy leads Jihli to realize that her dream represented not simply an egg hunt, but the rising hopes for the future.

Thanks to your efforts, Hatching–tide festivities may now begin in earnest, but given the resiliency of troublesome traps, you will never lack for leafkin to trim. Stop by for Sprig Cleaning whenever you have a moment, and enjoy the rest of these spring celebrations.

53: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)<Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! How good to see you again. You recognize me, yes? It's me, Jihli Aliapoh!
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)That's the spirit! How lovely to see you again, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. You remember me, yes? It's Jihli Aliapoh, come with another vision to share!

55: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)Why, thank you! These adorable accessories have been exclusively prepared for this year's festivities.
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)Why, these adorable accessories have been exclusively prepared for this year's festivities!

56: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)I am glad you have joined us again <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, and I do hope you will enjoy a happy Hatching–tide!
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)It is ever so good to have you with us again, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Happy Hatching–tide!

58: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)Jihli! Jihli Aliapoh! ...Oh, is this the first you've seen me without my eggshell cap? Goodness, for a moment I feared you had forgotten me!
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)Jihli! Jihli Aliapoh! ...Oh, is this the first you've seen me without my cap? For a moment, I feared you had forgotten me!

63: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)As we speak, our Dreamers are hiding eggs in the Black Shroud for participants to seek out and raise from the shrubbery, thereby reenacting my dream! Successful seekers will be awarded with <Emphasis>very</Emphasis> stylish glasses.

(-Jihli Aliapoh-)As we speak, our Dreamers are hiding eggs in the Black Shroud for participants to lift from the shrubbery, thereby reenacting my dream! Successful seekers will be awarded with <Emphasis>very</Emphasis> stylish glasses.

67: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)Thank you! I hope she has returned safely from our egg arena. It would be worth checking the aetheryte plaza before venturing further afield.
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)Thank you! I hope she has returned to Gridania safely. It would be worth checking the aetheryte plaza before venturing further afield.

68: (-Jihli Aliapoh-)Like me, she should be showing off a pair of our thematic glasses─hers in the shape of a chicken!
(-Jihli Aliapoh-)She, too, should be wearing a pair of our thematic glasses─hers in the shape of a chicken!

69: Were her spectacles created before mine, you ask? ...While I am uncertain whether the chicken preceeded the egg, Nonotta chose the former. I would check the aetheryte plaza for her before searching elsewhere!

Were her spectacles created before mine, you ask? ...While I am uncertain as to whether the chicken preceded the egg, Nonotta chose the former. I would check the aetheryte plaza for her before searching elsewhere!

71: My glasses? Why, they're a prize for participating in this year's Hatching–tide festivities─
My glasses? They're a prize for participating in this year's Hatching–tide festivities, but─

74: Why if it isn't <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! A happy Hatching–tide to you. How can I be of service?
Oh, hello, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! A happy Hatching–tide to you. How can I be of service?

75: Jihli sent you? I suppose I have been gone for awhile, but I've been having trouble hiding our eggs in the arena...
Jihli sent you? I suppose I have been gone for a while, but I've been having trouble hiding our eggs in the arena...

76: As I was working, these thick, tall grasses suddenly sprung forth! I tried to trim them back, but not even my best gardening scythe could make the smallest impression. At best, I managed to snip off a couple of leaves...

As I was working, these tall, thick grasses suddenly sprung forth! I tried to trim them back, but not even my best garden scythe could make the smallest impression. At best, I managed to snip a couple of leaves...

77: I cannot help but conclude that our arena is now carpeted in a peculiar type of leafkin that may pose a threat to our egg-hunting participants. I know this is all very sudden, but would you be willing to help me resolve this situation?

Now, I cannot help but worry that our arena is carpeted not by typical brambles, but a variety of <Emphasis>leafkin</Emphasis>... We can hardly send our egg hunters into a nest of monsters! Oh, what to do...

78: You have my deepest thanks! While I'm sure it is in an adventurer's blood to slay monsters first and ask questions later, I think we ought to gather more information on the plantlife in question before attempting to conquer it.
You would help? A godssend, truly! Then let us start by gathering information about this peculiar plant─though I know adventurers generally prefer to slay monsters first and ask questions later.

79: I shall head for the Botanists' Guild. In the meantime, if you could ask around the Carline Canopy for any who might know of similar flora or leafkin, I would greatly appreciate it.
I shall head for the Botanists' Guild. In the meantime, if you could ask around the Carline Canopy regarding the strange specimen, I would greatly appreciate it.

80: Here is one of the pieces I managed to shred from the odd stalks, for reference. Let's reconvene at the aetheryte plaza once we have gathered what information we can.
Here, take one of the leaves I managed to salvage. Let us reconvene at the aetheryte plaza once we have gathered what information we can.

81: You found Nonotta? Oh, good! And here I was, fearing some monstrous, eggy accident... I am glad the two of you are working together. You have my thanks!
Thank goodness Nonotta is hale and whole! Here I was fearing some monstrous, eggy accident... Thank you for your assistance!

84: We often receive petitions regarding roselets, but I don't believe I've seen leafkin of this particular hue before.
We often receive petitions regarding roselets, but I don't believe I've seen leafkin of this <Emphasis>particular</Emphasis> shade of green. Curious...

85: Just as typical plantlife flourishes during this season, so too do leafkin. I would not be surprised if some aberrant strain had developed in the process. If you require assistance in culling it, do not hesitate to ask.

Just as typical plant life flourishes during this season, so too do leafkin. I would not be surprised if some aberrant strain had developed in the process. If you require assistance in culling it, do not hesitate to ask.

94: That leaf may belong to a mutation. If it gives you any trouble, we can offer you help.
That leaf may belong to a mutation. If it gives you any trouble, we can help.

99: So others have been similarly unsuccessful in cutting them back? In which case, coming up with an alternative strategy does seem in order...
So others have been similarly unsuccessful in cutting them back? In which case, developing an alternative strategy does seem in order...

100: The alchemist you mentioned might be a good lead to follow up on, don't you think? An Elezen with shaded spectacles...perhaps that was the fellow with whom I was speaking before.
The alchemist you mentioned seems like a good lead, don't you think? An Elezen with shaded spectacles...perhaps that was the fellow with whom I was speaking earlier.

102: This is him! The gentleman I was speaking with earlier!
Here he is! The gentleman who asked about my glasses!

104: (-Herberin-)Yes, that sounds like me! Ravenous for leafkin large and small, the wayfayer alchemist Herberin, at your service.
(-Herberin-)Yes, that sounds like me! Ravenous for leafkin large and small, the wayfaring alchemist Herberin, at your service.

105:
(-Herberin-)Wha...aaa...aaat!? Why, this is the very leaf I have been pursuing for moons upon moons!
Moons upon moons upon moons upon moons!

(-Herberin-)Wha...aaa...aaat!? Why, this is the very leaf I have been pursuing for moons upon moons!
Moons upon moons upon moons upon <Emphasis>moooooons</Emphasis>!

106: (-Herberin-)...That is to say, if you are seeking assistance in combatting this menace, I would be happy to help.
(-Herberin-)...That is to say, if you are seeking assistance in combating this menace, I would be happy to help.

108: (-Herberin-)Ohoho! Doubt you not the efficacy of my concotions, little chicken. With this aberrant leaf in his possession, Herberin the Great can finally realize his purpose for traveling to the Twelveswood─can finally seize his quarry by the stalk and wrestle it into submission!

(-Herberin-)Ohoho! Doubt you not the efficacy of my concoctions, little chicken. With this aberrant leaf in his possession, Herberin the Great can finally realize his purpose for traveling to the Twelveswood─can finally seize his quarry by the stalk and wrestle it into submission!

109: (-Herberin-)...But my enthusiasm overcomes me. You see, as I was poring over beauties of the leafkin persuasion, my eyeballs were subjected to quite a piece of mischief. Since then, they burn like all seven hells if exposed to sunlight, wind, or mostly anything.

(-Herberin-)...But my enthusiasm overcomes me. You see, as I was poring over leafkin of this very persuasion, my eyeballs were subjected to quite a piece of mischief. Since then, they burn like all seven hells if exposed to sunlight, wind, or most anything.

110: (-Herberin-)So long as I wear these tinted lenses, I can remain upright and free of blinding pain and nausea for several bells at a time! Quite long enough to brew a tincture that shall spare you a similar fate. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have ingredients to procure from the Shaded Bower.

(-Herberin-)So long as I wear these tinted lenses, I can remain upright and free of blinding pain and nausea for several bells at a time! Quite long enough to brew a tincture that shall spare you both a similar fate. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have ingredients to procure from the Shaded Bower.

115: Therefore! By thwarting its regenerative effects, we may cut this terror down to size in not only the metaphorical sense, but the literal!
Therefore! By thwarting its regenerative properties, we may cut this terror down to size not only in the metaphorical sense, but the <Emphasis>literal</Emphasis>!

121: Not to mention all the free samples of plantlife to be gained along the way! Were I not liable to keel over at any moment, I would like nothing better than to slice the shrubbery by your side. However, I have every confidence that a brave adventurer such as yourself will succeed where I have failed.

Not to mention all the free samples of plant life to be gained along the way! Were I not liable to keel over at any moment, I would like nothing better than to slice the shrubbery by your side. However, I have every confidence that a brave adventurer such as yourself will succeed where I have failed.

122: Now, let us commence the marriage of alchemical marvels! Make me the happiest man in the realm, and bestow upon me your leaves!
Now, let us commence! Make me the happiest man in the realm, and bestow upon me your leaves!

125: Hnghhh... I offer up...my masterpiece...my soul...my well lubricated garden scythe...

Hnghhh...
I offer up...my masterpiece...my soul...my well-lubricated garden scythe...

126: In your hands, may it hack away our troubles with abandon...
In your hands, may it hack away our hardships...

131: L-Let's not get too carried away now, shall we!? Think of the children...
L-Let's not get too carried away, shall we!? Think of the children...

132: Now that our garden scythe is equipped to cut back the flytraps, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/> should be able to make our egg arena safe for all.
Now that our garden scythe is equipped to cut back the flytraps, we are one step closer to reclaiming our arena.

134: Herberin the Upright would like to join this rendezvous to observe the effects of his alchemical miracle. Insofar as they can be observed with scalded eyeballs.
Herberin the Upright would like to join this sortie to observe the effects of his alchemical miracle. Insofar as they can be observed with scalded eyeballs.

137: Oooh, I cannot wait to see those beautiful fronds again! May sprigs fall like rain beneath your blade, good <If(PlayerParameter(4))>lady<Else/>sir</If>.
Oooh, I cannot wait to see those beautiful fronds fall like rain beneath your blade!

138: Ready to clean our arena of troublesome sprigs and stalks?
Ready to clear our arena of troublesome sprigs and stalks?

139: Just say the word, and an egg usher shall whisk us away in our carriage!
Just say the word, and an egg usher shall whisk us away!

141: Wield a special gardening scythe to slay troublesome traps and retrieve Archon eggs. All classes and jobs are eligible to participate in Sprig Cleaning.
Wield a special garden scythe to slay troublesome traps and retrieve Archon eggs. All classes and jobs are eligible to participate in Sprig Cleaning.

142: Speak with an egg usher in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre or the Acorn Orchard to travel to the egg arena.
Speak with the egg ushers in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre or the Acorn Orchard to travel to the arena.

143: An egg usher will take us to the arena when you're ready.
When you're ready, just speak with an egg usher!

147: Not to mention sprigs of a most troublesome sort of trap! How I shall probe, how I shall stare─all without risk to what remains of my vision! Hoo hoo!
Not to mention sprigs of a most troublesome trap! How I shall probe, how I shall stare─all without risk to what remains of my vision! Hoo hoo!

149: I'm sure Jihli will be thrilled to hear that our egg arena is open for operation!
I'm sure Jihli will be thrilled to hear that our egg arena is operational!

150: Our arena was filled with leafkin!? No wonder preparations were taking longer than I expected. I am glad to hear the issue has been resolved and that everyone emerged unscathed!
Our arena was filled with leafkin!? No wonder preparations were taking longer than expected. I am glad to hear the issue has been resolved and that everyone emerged unscathed!

151: Not only did <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/> clean up the sprigs and sprouts that were crowding the area, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>she<Else/>he</If> also managed to retrieve what eggs were lost to the foliage. We should be ready to hide the eggs anew and begin the festivities!

Not only did <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/> clean up those sprigs and sprouts, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>she<Else/>he</If> also managed to retrieve what eggs were lost to the foliage. We should be ready to hide the eggs anew and begin the festivities!

152: I would like to thank the alchemist who was so crucial to the operation. Do you think Herberin is still in Gridania?
I would like to thank the alchemist who was so crucial to the operation. Do you think Master Herberin is still in Gridania?

156: From the leafkin samples you provided me, I was able to strengthen the regenerative properties of those flytraps into an ointment for my poor, blistered eyeballs. I am cured!
From the leafkin samples you provided me, I was able to concoct a regenerative ointment for my poor, blistered eyeballs. I am cured!

157: My habit of sticking my face too close to my object of study has, at long last, served me well. For where once venom splashed, so too did an unexpected antidote!
My habit of sticking my face too close to my object of study has, at long last, served me well. For where once venom splashed, so too did an antidote!

158: If extractions from these leaves can so easily counteract the leafkin's natural venom, can you imagine how else we might manipulate the properties of these fantastic creatures? We could develop cures upon cures, devise agents upon agents!
Can you imagine how else we might manipulate the properties of these fantastic creatures? We could develop cures upon cures, devise agents upon agents!

161: So it was you, my oblong comrades, who spoke of destiny. Had I but listened... Alas, Herberin has been a fool.
So it was you, my oval comrades, who spoke of destiny. Had I but listened... Alas, Herberin has been a fool.

162: But no longer! Thanks to you all, I am able to heed the wisdom of egg and continue my work with full faculties.
But no longer! I shall hereby heed the wisdom of Egg and continue my work with full faculties. All thanks to you!

165: It does me glad to see how Hatching–tide brings joy to one and all─and our celebrations have but scarcely begun!
It does me proud to see how Hatching–tide brings joy to one and all─and our celebrations have but scarcely begun!

166: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/> please accept these glasses as a token of our appreciation. You have more than earned them.
<Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, please accept these glasses as a token of our appreciation. You have more than earned them.

167: If those leafkin are half as resilient as you say they are, our arena may need further trimming... If you have the time to spare, we have more dazzling Hatching–tide prizes on offer!
Our Dreamers will continue patrols of the arena, but we may well need some further trimming. Your assistance would be much appreciated─and rewarded with special Hatching–tide gifts!

168: You can participate in Sprig Cleaning as many times as you wish during the seasonal event period by speaking with the egg ushers in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre or the Acorn Orchard.
You can participate in Sprig Cleaning as many times as you wish by speaking with the egg ushers in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre or the Acorn Orchard.

169:
これ以降の挑戦では、ステージが無制限に続きます。
制限時間内に次々と草を刈り、ハイスコアを狙ってみましょう！
Race against the clock as you slash your way through endless stages of shrubbery, and aim for the highest score possible!
